cards.front: What is a singly linked list?
cards.back: A data structure in which each element points to the next element.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of inserting a node at the beginning of a singly linked list?
cards.back: O(n), where n is the number of nodes in the list.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the next pointer in a singly linked list?
cards.back: To point to the next node in the list.

cards.front: What is a self-referential linked list?
cards.back: A linked list that contains pointers to its own nodes.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of deleting a node from a singly linked list?
cards.back: O(n), where n is the number of nodes in the list.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the null pointer in a linked list?
cards.back: To indicate the end of the list.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of traversing a singly linked list?
cards.back: O(n), where n is the number of nodes in the list.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of inserting a node at the end of a singly linked list?
cards.back: O(1), because only the last pointer needs to be updated.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of deleting the head node of a singly linked list?
cards.back: O(1), because only the head pointer needs to be updated.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the delete operation in a linked list?
cards.back: To remove a node from the list.

cards.front: What is the time complexity of searching for a node in a singly linked list?
cards.back: O(n), where n is the number of nodes in the list.

cards.front: What is the purpose of the length operation in a linked list?
cards.back: To get the number of nodes in the list.
